title: crictl config fails to create a missing explicit config file
author: immanuwell
body:
#### What happened:

`crictl --config /tmp/crictl.yaml config --set debug=true` fails if the file does not exist yet, even though the `config` command is supposed to bootstrap it.

#### What you expected to happen:

The command should create the file and write the requested setting.

#### How to reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible):

```bash
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
crictl --config "$tmpdir/crictl.yaml" config --set debug=true
```

Current result is a `get server config` error before the `config` command runs.

#### Anything else we need to know?:

This is a normal local CLI workflow. No special scale or boundary conditions are needed.

#### Environment:

Any host where `crictl` is run with a custom config path that does not exist yet.

title: Backward compatibility is broken because of cri-api change
author: bitoku
body:
Due to this recent change (https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/139168#issuecomment-4501163149), crictl no longer accepts environment variables in plain text; it now requires them to be base64-encoded.

Here is a reproducer: https://gist.github.com/bitoku/749684a85b44018e7d5cd4c77cd47202

The question is: do we want to fix this incompatibility or just leave it as is?
The downside of keeping the compatibility is that it may require some custom unmarshalling and that we won't be able to test non-UTF8 binaries using crictl.

title: dependabot for release branches
author: SergeyKanzhelev
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

#### What would you like to be added:

Release branches for all supported version and add dependabot configuration for currently supported branches.

#### Why is this needed:

As security becoming a higher priority with AI expansion, keeping the up to date release branches is more important then before. Even though downstream support their own branches, keeping those upstream will make it easier for dependant OSS projects to consume safer binaries.

title: Untrusted impersonation of the project at crictl.com
author: zerobitflux
body:
## ⚠️ Warning: This project is being impersonated

An untrusted website at `crictl.com` is impersonating this project. The site poses as an official resource. While it currently links to GitHub for downloads, **the site operator can replace these links with malicious files at any moment** without warning.

### What the site does

- Copies the project name, description, and branding to appear legitimate
- Currently links to GitHub downloads, but these links are **under the site operator's control and can be swapped out at any time**
- May collect user data or serve tampered downloads in the future

### How this was discovered

This domain was found as part of a broader investigation into a network of impersonation sites targeting open-source projects. Many of these sites share the same infrastructure and template.

### Recommended actions

For steps on how to report this site and request a DMCA takedown, see:
**https://gist.github.com/zerobitflux/57f3791086e18481030df9c204b323fe**

---

*This issue was created to alert maintainers. If you believe this was filed in error, please close it with a comment.*

title: critest for NRI integration
author: SergeyKanzhelev
body:
NRI is being used more and more. I am trying to understand where the NRI integration testing must reside. Does it belong to NRI repository or we should define some runtime conformance using critest.

I special case NRI because it is becoming increasingly part of a Pod lifecycle. CNI behavior may be considered the same way. And it will be a fair question, so let's discuss in this issue.

Examples of tests:

### NRI is actually called

Validating that NRI plugin is actually called with the proper arguments. This seems to be something that NRI may test for already.

### failure handling

Various tests where NRI plugin fails on sandbox and container creation as well as removal. Keeping the sandbox around if removal failed seems like a good conformance test. Same as propagating NRI errors up to creation calls. The key here is what runtime returns on NRI failure.

BTW, is there an ordering guarantee and whether NRI plugin which ACK-ed the creation of sandbox will be notified that the sandbox is NOT being created because of a second NRI plugin.

### allowing the retry

The retry must be allowed by runtime immidiately after create sandgox has failed with NRI error. This should ensure that sandbox is cleaned up before create sandbox CRI returned.

###  re-entrance consistency/invalid arguments handling

NRI must not be called when invalid arguments were passed in creation commands. Like creating the "same" sandbox that already exists or some bad names. Container creation for non-existing sandbox should not result in NRI call.

BTW, we do not have a good set of critests for bad arguments handling today. It feels like a good candidate for additional testing.

### NRI must block the sandbox creation for real

Long running NRI plugin on sandbox creation should be treated as sandbox was not created yet. So it should not be returned when you list sandboxes not creation of containers should not be allowed on it.

### ordering of things created

This is tricky to validate things in https://github.com/containerd/nri/pull/286/ - like whether cgroup hierarchy already created and such. This may be part of NRI testing. But we should seriously consider what parts of it are ok to adopt as conformance

title: Remediate CVE-2026-33186 (Critical)
author: dvasilen
body:
Scan Parameter | Value
--- | ---
architecture | amd64 
issueType | vulnerability 
severity | critical 
severityCHML | C 
cvss | 9.1 
riskFactors | Critical severity,Has fix,Recent vulnerability 
cve | CVE-2026-33186 
hasFix | Y 
status | fixed in 1.79.3 
packageType | go 
packageName | google.golang.org/grpc 
packageVersion | v1.79.2 
packageLicense |  
packageBinaryPkgs |  
packagePath | crictl 
description | ### Impact _What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_  It is an **Authorization Bypass** resulting from **Improper Input Validation** of the HTTP/2 `:path` pseudo-header.  The gRPC-Go server was too lenient in its routing logic, accepting requests where the `:path` omitted the mandatory leading slash (e.g., `Service/Method` instead of `/Service/Method`). While the server successfully routed these requests to the correct handler, authorization interceptors (including the official `grpc/authz` package) evaluated the raw, non-canonical path string. Consequently, 'deny' rules defined using canonical paths (starting with `/`) failed to match the incoming request, allowing it to bypass the policy if a fallback 'allow' rule was present.  **Who is impacted?** This affects gRPC-Go servers that meet both of the following criteria: 1. They use path-based authorization interceptors, such as the official RBAC implementation in `google.golang.org/grpc/authz` or custom interceptors relying on `info.FullMethod` or `grpc.Method(ctx)`. 2. Their security policy contains specific 'deny' rules for canonical paths but allows other requests by default (a fallback 'allow' rule).  The vulnerability is exploitable by an attacker who can send raw HTTP/2 frames with malformed `:path` headers directly to the gRPC server.  ### Patches _Has the problem been patched? What versions should users upgra

title: crictl images not filtering by repository:tag
author: MikeSpreitzer
body:
<!-- Please use this template while reporting a bug and provide as much info as possible. Not doing so may result in your bug not being addressed in a timely manner. Thanks!

If the matter is security related, please disclose it privately via https://kubernetes.io/security/
-->

#### What happened:
I tried to use `crictl images $repository:$tag` to test for the presence of a particular image, but the output was all the images rather than just the requested one.

```console
root@fmatest-control-plane:/# crictl images
IMAGE                                                     TAG                  IMAGE ID            SIZE
docker.io/kindest/kindnetd                                v20250214-acbabc1a   ee75e27fff91c       35.7MB
docker.io/kindest/local-path-helper                       v20241212-8ac705d0   b530d82c16da9       2.95MB
...

root@fmatest-control-plane:/# crictl images docker.io/kindest/kindnetd:v20250214-acbabc1a
IMAGE                                                     TAG                  IMAGE ID            SIZE
docker.io/kindest/kindnetd                                v20250214-acbabc1a   ee75e27fff91c       35.7MB
docker.io/kindest/local-path-helper                       v20241212-8ac705d0   b530d82c16da9       2.95MB
...
```

#### What you expected to happen:
I expected to get a list containing only the given repository:tag.

#### How to reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible):
Have multiple images. Use `crictl images $repo:$tag` to ask for just one.

#### Anything else we need to know?:

```console
root@fmatest-control-plane:/# crictl --version
crictl version 1.32.0
```

#### Environment:

- Container runtime or hardware configuration: `kind` version 0.27.0 on the host
- Guest OS (e.g: `cat /etc/os-release`): Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)
- Kernel (e.g. `uname -a`):
    - guest: `Linux fmatest-control-plane 6.6.119-0-virt #1-Alpine SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC 2025-12-10 08:04:08 aarch64 GNU/Linux`
    - host: `Darwin mjs13 24.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 24.6.0: Mon Jan 19 21:56:28 PST 2026; root:xnu-11417.140.69.708.3~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020 arm64`
- Others: Kubernetes v1.32.2 in the `kind` cluster

title: Add incremental checkpointing to crictl
author: GGh41th
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

#### What would you like to be added:
Add incremental checkpointing to crictl using criu pre-dumps.

This would look like this:
   crictl checkpoint --parent-path /path/to/pre-dumps-dir CONTAINER-ID 


#### Why is this needed:
crictl makes it easy to checkpoint containers, it would be nice for users who want to play with incremental checkpoiting to be able to do that with crictl.

title: Support global wildcard registry mirror / fallback
author: VrindaMarwah
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

#### What would you like to be added:

Allow wildcard / default entry such as:

[[registry]]
prefix = "*"
location = ""

[[registry.mirror]]
location = "my.internal.registry:5000"

or similar semantic default behavior.

#### Why is this needed:

Currently crio.conf requires explicit registry prefixes (docker.io, quay.io, etc) to configure mirrors.
There is no way to specify a global fallback mirror for arbitrary registries.

In air-gapped / enterprise environments we often operate a pull-through cache or private mirror and want:
- Try original registry
- If unreachable → fall back to internal mirror

for ANY registry prefix.

Today this requires mentioning every possible upstream registry and its mirror, which is not scalable.

title: Add validation tests for image volumes with user namespaces
author: AutuSnow
body:
<!-- Please only use this template for submitting feature requests -->

#### What would you like to be added:
Add validation tests for image volumes with user namespaces to ensure CRI implementations correctly handle idmap mounts for image volumes when pods use user namespaces.
#### Why is this needed:
container images as read-only volumes. When using user namespaces , the file ownership in these volumes needs to be correctly mapped using idmap mounts so that files appear with the correct ownership inside the container's user namespace.
﻿
Without proper idmap support, files in image volumes would appear with incorrect ownership inside containers using user namespaces, potentially breaking applications that rely on specific file permissions.
https://github.com/containerd/containerd/pull/12816

title: crictl ps output issue
author: lucming
body: Executing `crictl ps` in the terminal produces the following output: The `POD ID` column can be easily confused with `Pod UID`, when it actually displays the Pod's sandbox ID. Should we rename this column to `SANDBOX ID`?

title: Release v1.35.0
author: saschagrunert
body:
Targeting for Wednesday, Dec 10.

cc @kubernetes-sigs/cri-tools-maintainers 

#### Release notes

```markdown
## Changes by Kind

### Documentation

- Updated `docs/validation.md` do contain the most recent additions and changes. (#1940, @saschagrunert)
- Updated the docs to include missing features and fixes. (#1938, @saschagrunert)

### Failing Test

- Updated the Windows test image Dockerfile to use the ltsc2022. (#1952, @saschagrunert)

### Other (Cleanup or Flake)

- Containerd 1.6 is EOL and is not being tested any longer. (#1928, @SergeyKanzhelev)

## Dependencies

### Added
- cyphar.com/go-pathrs: v0.2.1
```

title: crictl v1.34.0 has a critical vulnerability - CVE-2025-22871
author: mabilgen
body:
crictl v1.34.0 has a critical CVE-2025-22871,
The current public version (1.34.0) is built with a vulnerable stdlib (go1.23.3) and is affected by CVE-2025-22871.

```
=== CRITICAL SEVERITY VULNERABILITIES ===

Vulnerability: CVE-2025-22871
  Severity:      Critical
  Package:       stdlib@go1.23.3
  Package Path:  /usr/local/bin/crictl
  Fix Available: 1.23.8,1.24.2
  CVSS Score:    9.1
  URL:           https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-22871
  Detected:      2025-10-10T23:59:09Z
```

title: `crictl rmi` removes all images with the same image id instead of the specified tag
author: nirs
body:
#### What happened:

Trying to debug minikube bug with removing images, turns out the `minikube image rm` uses `crictl rmi image:tag` under the hood.

So I have 3 images, created using:

```console
$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io pull docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0
docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0:                                                resolved       |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 
index-sha256:127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6:    done           |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 
manifest-sha256:42a89d9b22e5307cb88494990d5d929c401339f508c0a7e98a4d8ac52623fc5b: done           |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 
config-sha256:ce2d2cda2d858fdaea84129deb86d18e5dbf1c548f230b79fdca74cc91729d17:   done           |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 
layer-sha256:ac2c07efdce850736ed8e7b9c3bc15e251d808fc43b9eeeb88797be0def23730:    done           |++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++| 
elapsed: 3.4 s                                                                    total:  2.4 Ki (725.0 B/s)                                       

$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io image tag docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0 localhost/test-base:latest

$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io image tag localhost/test-base:latest localhost/test-tag:latest

$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io image ls | grep 127ac38a2bb9
localhost/test-tag                         latest                127ac38a2bb9    8 seconds ago     linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
localhost/test-base                        latest                127ac38a2bb9    19 seconds ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
<none>                                     <none>                127ac38a2bb9    31 seconds ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
kicbase/echo-server                        1.0                   127ac38a2bb9    31 seconds ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
```

The purpose of the images is to test minikube image commands. For every test we want to create a new tag and preform an operation on the tag, like deleting it. The other images should bot be affected. This allows running tests in parallel since every test operates on its own image.

The unexpected hehavior - when deleting one tag, all the images with the same image id are deleted:

```console
$ sudo crictl --debug rmi localhost/test-tag:latest
DEBU[0000] Get image connection                         
DEBU[0000] User specified image to be removed: localhost/test-tag:latest 
DEBU[0000] ImageStatusRequest: image:{image:"localhost/test-tag:latest"} 
DEBU[0000] ImageStatusResponse: image:{id:"sha256:ce2d2cda2d858fdaea84129deb86d18e5dbf1c548f230b79fdca74cc91729d17"  repo_tags:"docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0"  repo_tags:"localhost/test-base:latest"  repo_tags:"localhost/test-tag:latest"  size:1977221} 
DEBU[0000] RemoveImageRequest: image:{image:"localhost/test-tag:latest"} 
Deleted: docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0
Deleted: localhost/test-base:latest
Deleted: localhost/test-tag:latest
```

#### What you expected to happen:

Only the tag "localhost/test-tag:latest" is deleted.

This works as expected with both `ctr image rm` and `nerdcl image rm`.

ctr:

```console
$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io image rm localhost/test-tag:latest
localhost/test-tag:latest

$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io image ls | grep 127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6
docker.io/kicbase/echo-server:1.0                                       application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json sha256:127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6 1.9 MiB   linux/amd64,linux/arm64                                                      io.cri-containerd.image=managed                                 
localhost/test-base:latest                                              application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json sha256:127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6 1.9 MiB   linux/amd64,linux/arm64                                                      io.cri-containerd.image=managed                                 
sha256:ce2d2cda2d858fdaea84129deb86d18e5dbf1c548f230b79fdca74cc91729d17 application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json sha256:127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6 1.9 MiB   linux/amd64,linux/arm64                                                      io.cri-containerd.image=managed                                 
```

nerdctl:

```console
$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io image rm localhost/test-tag:latest
Untagged: localhost/test-tag:latest@sha256:127ac38a2bb9537b7f252addff209ea6801edcac8a92c8b1104dacd66a583ed6
Deleted: sha256:5d26d12357a043b2446bf19e036f55cee8454728871f8dbe007f3127cd22d649

$ sudo nerdctl -n k8s.io image ls | grep 127ac38a2bb9
localhost/test-base                        latest                127ac38a2bb9    4 minutes ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
<none>                                     <none>                127ac38a2bb9    4 minutes ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
kicbase/echo-server                        1.0                   127ac38a2bb9    4 minutes ago    linux/arm64    4.792MB    1.976MB
```

#### How to reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible):

As explained above. Reproducible with both
- minikube master (using containerd 1.17.23)
- minikube master with containerd v2.1.4 (https://github.com/kubernetes/minikube/pull/21409)

#### Anything else we need to know?:

#### Environment:

- Container runtime or hardware configuration:

- OS (e.g: `cat /etc/os-release`):

```
$ cat /etc/os-release

VERSION=2025.02-dirty

```

- Kernel (e.g. `uname -a`):

```
$ uname -a
Linux minikube 6.6.95 #1 SMP PREEMPT Tue Sep  2 22:29:31 UTC 2025 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

- Others:

Note: the minikube image mention comes with crictl 1.28.0. I installed crictl 1.34.0 manually to check if the issue is related to the old version. The behavior is the same.

title: Release cri-tools v1.34.0
author: saschagrunert
body: Planned for Aug 21, right after Kubernetes v1.34.0-rc.1.
